Kuwait’s foreign ministry has issued a statement condemning the Iranian attacks against the country. It reads almost identical to the statement issued yesterday, although emphasises Kuwait’s sovereignty is “a red line”.

“The state of Kuwait reserves its full rights to take all necessary measures to protect its security and preserve its sovereignty,” it said.

Continue reading...

events.title: US launches new airstrikes against Iran hours after Trump threatens to escalate
events.summary:
Iran responds by targeting US-allied Kuwait and Qatar and accusing US of striking near its sole nuclear power plant

The US has launched new airstrikes against Iran, hours after Donald Trump threatened to escalate the conflict unless Iran stopped attacking ships in the strait of Hormuz.

Iran, which was burying its former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on Thursday, responded by targeting US-allied Kuwait and Qatar and accused the US of striking near its sole nuclear power plant.

Continue reading...

events.title: Andy Burnham apologises for Labour’s stance on Gaza and says it ‘didn’t get it right’
events.summary:
Exclusive: PM-in-waiting says party must ‘do better’ in approach to Middle East and he will put more pressure on Israel

Andy Burnham has apologised for Labour’s initial response to Israel’s military action in Gaza, saying the party “didn’t get it right” and needs to “do better” under his leadership signalling a significant shift in the UK’s approach to the Middle East.

The prime minister-in-waiting told the Guardian he would put more pressure on the Israeli government, including through further sanctions on individuals and entities, as well as a potential ban on the trade of goods with illegal settlements.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘It was back-to-back explosions’: Iranians recall 48 hours of terror after US attack on port cities
events.summary:
Residents in Bandar Abbas and Sirik regions describe communities in fear and ‘feeling abandoned’ after US strikes

  • Middle East crisis live – latest updates

Residents in southern Iran have described two nights of fear this week after explosions shook coastal communities as the US launched fresh strikes on targets including the port cities of Bandar Abbas and Sirik.

US Central Command confirmed the attacks, saying they were carried out to “further degrade their ability to threaten freedom of navigation in the strait of Hormuz”.

Continue reading...

events.title: US charges Indian criminal gang leader with organising murder of Canadian Sikh activist
events.summary:
Lawrence Bishnoi, who is in prison in India, is accused of orchestrating assassination of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in 2023

US and Canadian authorities say they have “dismantled” the leadership of a notorious Indian criminal group, charging dozens of operatives who have “inflicted pain and cruelty on people, victims around the globe”, including a high-profile murder in Canada that strained diplomatic relations between Canada and India.

At a press conference on Tuesday, members of the FBI and Canada’s Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) said as part of Operation Hard Ball – a multiyear federal investigation into murder-for-hire plots, shootings, extortion and drug trafficking – they had charged 37 people, some of whom were already in custody. Authorities are still searching for seven fugitives in the US, two in India and one in Europe.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pakistan locates wreckage of Boeing 737 cargo plane that went missing off coast
events.summary:
Early flight data shows K2 Airways plane crashed into sea with five crew on board south-west of Karachi

Pakistan has located the wreckage of a Boeing cargo plane, the country’s airports authority said, adding that rescuers were searching for the five crew members on board when the aircraft went missing.

The plane was approaching Karachi from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates when radar showed it “rapidly descending” on Tuesday evening after reporting a “navigational system issue”, according to the Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA).

Continue reading...

events.title: A rock star welcome and human rights protests to greet India’s prime minister Narendra Modi in Australia
events.summary:
Amnesty International says Albanese has opportunity to ‘reaffirm mutual commitment’ to rule of law as thousands in diaspora expected at stadium event

  • Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

The Indian prime minister Narendra Modi’s first visit to Australia in three years has sparked calls for the federal government to raise human rights concerns, as excitement builds within the nation’s Indian diaspora.

Modi’s visit begins on Wednesday evening and marks his third since becoming prime minister. It will see him return to Melbourne after more than a decade, with a stadium event expected to attract more than 20,000 people.

Continue reading...

events.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
events.summary:
Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961

Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research.

The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases.

Continue reading...

events.title: Sri Lanka prison riot kills 26, with more than 100 others wounded
events.summary:
Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries rushed to hospital after fighting between prisoners from two drug gangs

Clashes at a Sri Lankan jail have killed 26 people, including seven guards, and wounded more than 100 in the country’s deadliest prison riot in years, officials said.

Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries were rushed to Negombo hospital, north of the capital Colombo after overnight fighting between prisoners from two drug gangs, police said on Monday.

Continue reading...

events.title: Keir Starmer may award resignation honours when he leaves No 10
events.summary:
Outgoing PM has not ruled out drawing up honours list when he stands down, despite previously opposing it

Keir Starmer has signalled he could hand out resignation honours when he leaves Downing Street, despite pledging three years ago he would not do so when he eventually stood down.

The prime minister twice declined to rule out drawing up a list of honours when he stands down in just over 10 days’ time, to be succeeded by Andy Burnham.

Continue reading...

events.title: Burnham’s apology over Gaza marks ‘reset moment’ as Labour seeks to win back progressive voters
events.summary:
It remains unclear how much of substance will change – and whether it will be enough to rebuild electoral coalition

On the final day of Labour’s party conference in 2023, when the public was still reeling from the brutal Hamas attack on Israel just days before, Keir Starmer took to the airwaves for the traditional broadcast round – but gave one interview that would have particularly damaging fallout.

Sitting down with LBC’s Nick Ferrari, the then opposition leader asserted Israel’s right to defend itself, a stance that was in line with the broad political consensus at the time. But then he also appeared to suggest it had “the right” to withhold power and water from Palestinian civilians.

Continue reading...

events.title: Graham Linehan gets £25,000 compensation and an apology from Met police after arrest
events.summary:
Father Ted co-creator detained by armed officers at Heathrow airport last year over gender-critical posts on X

The Father Ted co-creator Graham Linehan has been paid £25,000 in compensation by the Metropolitan police and received an apology after his arrest over his gender-critical social media posts.

Linehan, 57, was detained by armed officers at Heathrow airport last September after a 10-hour flight from Arizona.

Continue reading...

events.title: Doreen Lawrence will not foot any of legal bill for failed attempt to sue Daily Mail
events.summary:
Exclusive: Source close to co-claimant Prince Harry says he is protective of social campaigner and will not ‘see her out of pocket’

Doreen Lawrence, the social justice campaigner, will not foot any of a multimillion-pound bill for the failed attempt to sue the publisher of the Daily Mail, the Guardian understands.

Lawrence, the mother of the murdered teenager Stephen Lawrence, whose case was the subject of a long-running Daily Mail campaign from the late 1990s, was one of seven claimants defeated in court over claims the Mail titles used unlawful methods to source stories.

Continue reading...

events.title: Labour MPs to rebel on party funding reforms and demand total crypto ban
events.summary:
Exclusive: Call for ministers to tighten bill amid Nigel Farage funding controversies

Labour MPs are to rebel next week over the government’s reforms to political funding, pushing ministers to introduce tougher measures including a ban on cryptocurrency donations and much lower spending limits.

MPs on the all-party anti-corruption group are canvassing support for four amendments to the representation of the people bill that would significantly tighten the government’s plans.

Continue reading...

events.title: Maine says ‘no official withdrawal notice has yet been received’ from Graham Platner – live
events.summary:
Source tells the Guardian Platner intends to officially withdraw on Monday; Democrats have limited time to finalize an alternative Senate nominee in Maine

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

Former Olympic canoeist David Hearn has pleaded not guilty to vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool.

Hearn, 67, from Maryland, was arrested last month after stopping by the pool on a bike ride. He told several outlets that he was detained for almost five hours after he reached into the water to inspect what he described a piece of the blue liner that was partially detached from the bottom of the pool as he was curious what it felt like.

Continue reading...

events.title: Suspect in Charlie Kirk killing said ‘he wishes he hadn’t done it’, roommate testifies
events.summary:
On fourth day of preliminary hearing, state presents video of a prosecutor interviewing Lance Twiggs

Tyler Robinson, the 23-year-old charged with murdering far-right pundit Charlie Kirk, cried and told his then-roommate Lance Twiggs “he wishes he hadn’t done it” one day after the fatal shooting, according to testimony played Thursday in court.

On the fourth day of a hearing to determine whether the case has probable cause to proceed to trial, the state presented the video of a prosecutor interviewing Twiggs, after back-and-forth on Wednesday over whether broadcasting the remarks to the public could expose potential jurors to prejudicial information.

Continue reading...

events.title: US lawmakers urge stricter monitoring of medically assisted suicide in hospices
events.summary:
Bipartisan group warns HHS that older adults and people with disabilities risk being pressured to end their lives

Lawmakers urged the health and human services (HHS) secretary, Robert F Kennedy Jr, on Thursday to establish strict hospice reporting rules to prevent discrimination and coercion in medically assisted suicide.

The bipartisan group of members of Congress warned that older adults, people with disabilities, or those with disaffected caregivers face a particular risk of being pressured to end their lives.

Continue reading...

events.title: OpenAI releases latest ChatGPT model after delay over White House cybersecurity concerns
events.summary:
Staggered release of ChatGPT 5.6 follows similar restrictions on rival firm Anthropic’s latest AI models

OpenAI released its latest advanced AI model, called ChatGPT 5.6, on Thursday after earlier delaying the public rollout over US government concerns about cybersecurity. The Trump administration had requested last month that OpenAI limit the release to a small group of government-approved users.

OpenAI complied with the White House’s request last month. The company stated in a blogpost that it had briefed government officials on ChatGPT 5.6’s capabilities and restricted the model to trusted partners at their behest. The product’s wider release came after additional testing by the government’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation agency, according to Axios.

Continue reading...

events.title: Maine progressives race to find Platner replacement as centrists call for ‘normie Democrat’
events.summary:
Platner’s former backers are seeking ‘real progressive’ to prevent nomination going to establishment Democrat

  • US politics live – latest updates

Progressive groups and lawmakers who rallied behind Graham Platner’s insurgent bid for a US Senate seat are now racing to decide where to transfer their support after his withdrawal from the Maine race following yet another allegation of sexual assault.

The scramble and apparent heartbreak underscores the uncertainty facing the coalition surrounding Platner’s anti-establishment message, and the response from more centrist Democrats to proceed with caution. Organizations, voters, volunteers and elected officials that once saw him as a vehicle for a more populist progressive agenda are now weighing whether to unite behind a successor, or hold back until the party’s replacement process plays out.

Continue reading...

events.title: Former U.S. envoy reacts to Iran strikes and NATO summit
events.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.

events.title: Where are Iran and Ukraine wars headed after NATO summit?
events.summary: Fresh strikes between Iran and the United States and a tense ceasefire scrapped — Is the war with Iran back on?

events.title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
events.summary: Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.

events.title: How England's class divide shaped Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s likely next prime minister
events.summary: As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?

events.title: Egypt complains officials were biased in World Cup loss to Argentina
events.summary: The Egyptian Football Association (EFA) said Wednesday it "cannot remain silent" after what it believes was unfair and biased officiating in Egypt's 3-2 round of 16 loss against Argentina on Tuesday.

events.title: Trump wraps NATO summit on a positive note,  after meeting Zelenskyy
events.summary: President Trump capped a NATO summit in Turkey meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and saying that the U.S. will give Ukraine a license to make Patriot air defense systems.

events.title: The U.S. launches new airstrikes on Iran and Tehran fires back at Gulf Arab states
events.summary: The United States launched new airstrikes against Iran early Thursday, and Tehran responded by targeting U.S.-allied Mideast countries, raising fears of resuming war.

events.title: In a West Bank cave, Israelis and Palestinians hold an out-of-the-ordinary lunch
events.summary: In the occupied West Bank, Israeli settlers keep seizing land in areas where Palestinians have lived for generations. The conflict even reaches caves in remote corners of the territory.

events.title: Trump threatens Iran as NATO summit wraps
events.summary: President Trump threatened more strikes against Iran after saying he believes the ceasefire is dead at the end of the NATO summit.

events.title: New U.S. passports with Trump's image are available. Here's how to get (or avoid) one
events.summary: The passports feature an image of President Trump and are only available in person at the Washington Passport Agency — and only by appointment.

events.title: Mali rebels attack convoy of soldiers, Russian mercenaries
events.summary: The convoy was bringing hundreds of personnel in reinforcement in northern Mali, where the Malian army and Russian paramilitaries are fighting jihadist and separatist militants.

events.title: EU lawmakers pave way for 'digital euro' negotiations
events.summary: The digital euro has been touted as the EU's best chance at breaking its dependence on US payment systems like Visa and Mastercard, as well as Apple Pay and Google Pay.

events.title: Ukraine denies involvement in Nord Stream blasts
events.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general proposed forming a joint investigative team with Germany. Berlin has indicted a former Ukrainian soldier over the blasts and says he was working on behalf of Ukrainian entities.

events.title: Abbas sets Palestinian legislative elections for November
events.summary: The Palestinian Authority has announced it will soon hold its first elections in twenty years. However, questions remain about whether it has the means and legitimacy to do so.

events.title: South Africa: Driven home by anti-migrant protests
events.summary: Two women tell DW how anti-migrant protests upended their lives in South Africa, forcing them to return to Zambia and rebuild from scratch.

events.title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
events.summary: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

events.title: Syria regains voting rights at world chemical weapons watchdog
events.summary: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) cited a "change in circumstances" as the reason behind its decision. Syria had joined the watchdog in 2013, only to be stripped of its rights in 2021.

events.title: How do Germans cope with having Nazi grandparents?
events.summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

events.title: Congo: UN laments uptick in fighting in South Kivu
events.summary: The UN human rights chief has called for an immediate halt to fighting in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. This follows intensified clashses between the army and the Rwanda-backed M23 militia in South Kivu.

events.title: China: Dozens killed in shoe factory fire
events.summary: Hundreds of rescue workers were dispatched to the site in Jinjiang, China, where many had become trapped in a burning factory. President Xi Jinping said "heavy human losses" were expected.

events.title: Iran war: Trump calls second set of US strikes 'retribution'
events.summary: The US launched fresh strikes on Iran overnight, the second wave of attacks in 24 hours after President Trump declared a ceasefire to be over. Meanwhile, Iran entombed slain leader Khamenei. DW has more.

events.title: Iran after Khamenei: A new order takes shape
events.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader, has inherited a political system that his father spent decades building and shaping in his own image.

events.title: Germany news: Man jailed over killing of train conductor
events.summary: A verdict has been delivered in a trial that drew national attention and raised concern about train staff safety. Meanwhile, VW bosses are eyeing huge restructuring, with unions vowing to fight any closures. DW has more.

events.title: US midterms: Democrat exits key Senate race after rape claim
events.summary: Democrat Graham Platner, an oysterman running for the US Senate in Maine, suspended his campaign after he was accused of sexual assault. The announcement throws a closely watched 2026 midterms race into uncertainty.

events.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
events.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

events.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' dies at 75
events.summary: The Welsh singer was best known for the 1980s rock opera hit, along with other songs, including "It's a Heartache."

events.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
events.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

events.title: Germany: June heat wave linked to 5,000 excess deaths
events.summary: Several other European Union countries reported thousands of additional deaths as June 2026 officially became the hottest on record for bloc.

events.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
events.summary: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

events.title: India news: Modi clinches uranium supply deal with Australia
events.summary: India's nuclear energy ambitions have received a fresh impetus from its Indo-Pacific partner Australia. The two nations have reached a deal to export Australian uranium to India. DW has more.

events.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
events.summary: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

events.title: Egypt's wrath after World Cup exit: 'The match was rigged'
events.summary: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

events.title: Germany reports 'shock' rise in drug deaths among under-30s
events.summary: The number of drug-related deaths remains at a record high in Germany. And the victims are getting younger and younger. Many deaths are related to new and increasingly dangerous substances such as synthetic opioids.

events.title: France's far right set to improve ties with Germany
events.summary: France’s right-wing populist Marine Le Pen is running for President. Her chances aren’t bad. Her party, the RN, is already reaching out to Germany.

events.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
events.summary: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

events.title: The Australian tree reshaping the world's wildfires
events.summary: As Europe experiences another summer of extreme heat and wildfire warnings, one tree imported from Australia is coming under renewed scrutiny across the world: eucalyptus.

events.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
events.summary: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.

events.title: Attacks on Kyiv ahead of NATO summit: What is Putin's goal?
events.summary: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

events.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
events.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

events.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
events.summary: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

events.title: Ukraine: Suspect in Monaco bomb attack found dead near Kyiv
events.summary: Ukrainain prosecutors say the body of the woman suspected of the bombing that targeted businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was found near Kyiv. Police said they had detained two individuals suspected of murdering her.

events.title: Mbappe hits back at racist insults from Paraguay senator
events.summary: A Paraguayan lawmaker has sparked outrage with racist insults directed at footballer Kylian Mbappe. The French FA says it will take legal action, and President Emmanuel Macron has expressed support for the player.

events.title: Germany: Record debt and a seismic policy shift
events.summary: The German government's draft budget for 2027 includes more money for the military and less for everything else, while debt continues to grow rapidly.

events.title: Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
events.summary: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

events.title: Why Volkswagen's huge workforce became a costly burden
events.summary: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

events.title: Why migrants come to Germany for work and then leave again
events.summary: Germany has long attracted skilled workers to its labor market. The country has been much less successful at retaining them. So what is Germany doing wrong?

events.title: Canada awards Germany major submarine contract
events.summary: Canadian PM Carney said the deal of up to 12 German-built submarines will be the "largest in Canadian history." Germany had pushed hard for the order, which comes as Canada seeks stronger ties with Europe.

events.title: Emmanuel Macron visits Syria, 18 months after Assad's fall
events.summary: The French president landed in Damascus on Monday evening. He's the first EU leader to visit the country since Bashar Assad's sudden downfall and the rise of former militant turned President Ahmed al-Sharaa.

events.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
events.summary: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
events.summary: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

events.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
events.summary: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

events.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
events.summary: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

events.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
events.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

events.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
events.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

events.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
events.summary: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

events.title: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
events.summary: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

events.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
events.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

events.title: Albania PM Rama: Protests a stage for anti-Trump forces
events.summary: Protesters in Albania say their government is selling the country its nature out to foreign investors and luxury developers. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama tells DW why he thinks people are so upset.

events.title: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
events.summary: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

events.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
events.summary: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

events.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
events.summary: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

events.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
events.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

events.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
events.summary: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

events.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
events.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

events.title: Jürgen Klopp and Germany: A partnership long in the making
events.summary: In one of the worst-kept secret in football, the former Dortmund and Liverpool coach looks to be on the verge of becoming Germany's new boss.

events.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
events.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

events.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
events.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

events.title: The downfall: Julian Nagelsmann out as Germany coach
events.summary: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
events.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

events.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
events.summary: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

events.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
events.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

events.title: German public university creates Islamic theology faculty
events.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish an Islamic theology faculty, a move that is is attracting international attention.

events.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
events.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

events.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
events.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

events.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
events.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

events.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
events.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

events.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
events.summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

events.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
events.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

events.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
events.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

events.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
events.summary: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.

events.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
events.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger takes over as Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have presented Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She is the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Hydration breaks: The most controversial issue at the World Cup
events.summary: The mandatory hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup have sparked debate in the stands, on the pitch, and far beyond.

events.title: German prosecutors arrest Rwanda genocide suspect
events.summary: A German-Rwandan national has been arrested on suspicion of complicity in murders during Rwanda's 1994 genocide. He is thought to have been personally involved in the killing of one victim.

events.title: Ahead of NATO summit, Turkey arrests more than 200 activists
events.summary: Protesters are clearly unwelcome ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. Amid a strict public gatherings ban, 225 human rights and environmental activists as well as journalists have already been arrested.

events.title: Digital euro: The plan to Trump-proof the EU's economies
events.summary: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

events.title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
events.summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the Trump administration is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

events.title: EU slaps €3 duty on low-value imported packages
events.summary: The EU has moved to curb what it sees as unfair competition from mostly Chinese online retailers by imposing its levy. It follows a similar move from the US.

events.title: Global firms and German startups compete in realizing nuclear fusion
events.summary: Companies around the world are competing to see who can build the first commercially viable nuclear fusion reactor. German startups are also in the race, supported by major corporations and private investors.

events.title: Germany: Heat wave is over, political fallout has just begun
events.summary: After a major heat wave, critics say leaders need to do more for climate change adaption. Policymakers disagree on who's responsible to prepare Germany for extreme temperatures ― the federal government or municipalities.

events.title: Angela Merkel unveils official portrait in Berlin
events.summary: On Tuesday former German Chancellor Merkel presented the painting for the official portrait gallery of the country's heads of government. She had previously inspired many artists.

events.title: Will alleged crime ties damage Serbia's ruling party?
events.summary: Pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic is growing as years of investigations into alleged links between the state and organized crime coincide with a gang war. Experts say the issue is eroding support within his base.

events.title: Portugal's 'golden visa' delays spark legal fight
events.summary: After waiting years for their "golden visas," investors are taking Portugal to court. Tougher citizenship rules have added fuel to the growing dispute.

events.title: Germany out of 2026 World Cup: What went wrong?
events.summary: Germany are out of the World Cup earlier than expected for the third straight time. How did this happen?

events.title: Germany out of World Cup after loss on penalties to Paraguay
events.summary: Germany lost in dramatic fashion against Paraguay in Boston to exit the 2026 World Cup at the first knockout stage.

events.title: Germany news: Temperatures plunge after historic heat wave
events.summary: A record-breaking heat wave has moved on, with temperatures sinking rapidly amid many storms. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is heading to Washington for talks with his US counterpart, with NATO issues in focus.

events.title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
events.summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

events.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
events.summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

events.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
events.summary: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

events.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
events.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

events.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari signs for Bayern Munich
events.summary: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, has signed for Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

events.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
events.summary: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

events.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
events.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

events.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
events.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

events.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
events.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

events.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
events.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

events.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
events.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

events.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
events.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

events.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
events.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

events.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
events.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

events.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
events.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

events.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
events.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

events.title: World Cup: Why Mexico may be LA's favorite team
events.summary: Mexican-American football fans in the USA often split allegiances between their two cultures. For many in Los Angeles, the Mexico national team trumps the nation they call home.

events.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
events.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

events.title: Curacao's trailblazing doctor at the World Cup
events.summary: Suzanne Huurman is the only female chief doctor at the 2026 World Cup. Her work is inspiring change in men's football.

events.title: Experts axed by Trump keep climate information flowing
events.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

events.title: Schweinsteiger's Ivory Coast comments spark racism debate
events.summary: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

events.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
events.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
events.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

events.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
events.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

events.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
events.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

events.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
events.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

events.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
events.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

events.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
events.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

events.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
events.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

events.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
events.summary: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

events.title: How cities are adapting to a warming world
events.summary: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

events.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
events.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

events.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
events.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

events.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
events.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

events.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
events.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

events.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
events.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

events.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
events.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

events.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
events.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

events.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
events.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

events.title: Leipzig Bach festival: Encouraging dialog
events.summary: Talking, debating and listening are skills we can all learn, particularly in the light of current global conflicts. The Leipzig Bachfest shows how Johann Sebastian Bach's music can serve as a model here.

events.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
events.summary: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't end shipping risks
events.summary: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But naval mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.

events.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?
events.summary: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.

events.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
events.summary: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

events.title: Swiss to vote on whether to cap population at 10 million
events.summary: On June 14, Swiss voters will be able to decide on a far-right initiative to curb future immigration. What will it mean for the economy — and the country's European neighbors — if they approve the proposal?

events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
events.summary: SpaceX floated on the Nasdaq stock exchange on Friday, with shares trading 25% above the opening price at one point. The IPO has made Musk the world's first trillionaire, at least on paper.

events.title: British artist David Hockney dies at 88
events.summary: Considered a superstar of pop art, the UK painter was one of the world's most influential artists during his lifetime.

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather bag fails to sell at auction
events.summary: The handbag promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product" didn't find a buyer at auction. Is it more chicken than dinosaur?

events.title: Preaching and teaching in a warming world
events.summary: US pastors, a Ugandan musician and salmon-friendly tech delivered good environmental news this week.

events.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
events.summary: Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

events.title: Musk's SpaceX IPO holds both promise and peril
events.summary: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and AI company, presents potential for investors. But its futuristic, unproven plans also carry significant financial risks.

events.title: Sex doll art sparks debate at German president's residence
events.summary: Beyond a headline-grabbing bronze, contemporary artworks draw attention to political power at an exhibition held at Bellevue Palace before the state building closes for renovation.

events.title: 'Disclosure Day': For Spielberg, aliens are not just fiction
events.summary: As Steven Spielberg releases a new film on extraterrestrials, here's a brief history of modern alien conspiracies and why scientists are now taking UFO reports seriously.

events.title: The climate conversation many US pastors avoid
events.summary: As the Trump administration undoes scores of environmental protections, some Christian leaders are inviting their congregations to do as the Bible asks and be good stewards of the land. But many others stay silent.

events.title: UN 'deeply concerned' by ongoing drone attack in Sudan
events.summary: In tonight's edition, the ICC  is laying down the groundwork for investigations into some of the atrocities of Sudan's brutal war. Also, rights workers, politicians, developers, and more are currently in Geneva for the AI for Good Summit. And South African artist Credo V. Daniel's music is removed from Apple after a  performance divides fans,  some accuse him of relying too much on AI for his vocals.

events.title: World Cup: ATFrenchies share their France-Morocco predictions
events.summary:
⚽ 🏆 Alexandre Durand and Tom Carles, the duo that make up AT Frenchies, are showing their support for France's #LesBleus as they face #Morocco in the first quarter final at the FIFA World Cup. 
 
🇫🇷 🇲🇦 Who do they think will win? They share their analysis with FRANCE 24 ▶️

events.title: Technical details need to be agreed before Ukraine can produce Patriot missiles, Zelensky says
events.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday said that technical details still needed to be finalised with the US before Kyiv could start manufacturing its own Patriot air-defence missiles. Ukraine's dwindling interceptor stockpiles have been outstripped by Russia's deadly ballistic missile attacks.

events.title: Nike and Adidas locked in World Cup standoff as tournament heats up
events.summary: As the World Cup quarterfinals get under way, the race to number one between Nike and Adidas heats up. India and Australia sign a new uranium deal. And, OpenAI rolls out its newest model GPT-5.6 Sol to the general public.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Fake racism videos go viral as players, fans face real abuse
events.summary: AI fake videos, viral hoaxes and real racism are colliding at the FIFA World Cup. From a fake AI-generated Ronald Koeman rant and false claims targeting Algerian fans to the racist abuse of Kylian Mbappé and FIFA investigations, misinformation is spreading alongside genuine discrimination. Vedika Bahl fact-checks the biggest World Cup racism claims, exposes the fake ones, and separates viral AI deception from real incidents of racist abuse on and off the pitch.

events.title: Gazans mourn aid worker killed by Israel who organised public World Cup screenings
events.summary: Crowds of Gazans took to the streets on Wednesday for the funeral of Palestinian aid worker Mohammad al-Waheidi, who was killed by an Israeli air strike Tuesday. Waheidi had been involved in setting up public screenings of World Cup matches in the devastated Palestinian territory.

events.title: Not built for the heat: How do cities adapt to record temperatures?
events.summary: It's not even Bastille Day yet, and France is already in the grip of its third heatwave of the season. Hospitals are under strain, wildfires are spreading, riverbeds are running dry, work and study are being disrupted—and there's even a shortage of ice. Many of you reacted strongly to our recent discussion on whether air conditioning is the answer. We'll briefly revisit that debate, but also look at the bigger picture: why is France still so unprepared for extreme heat?

events.title: France-Morocco live: Les Bleus aim for semi-finals in 2022 rematch against Atlas Lions
events.summary: France face Morocco in a mouth-watering quarter-final clash, with both nations seeking to reach the semi-finals of the World Cup 2026. Les Bleus continue their pursuit of another title with their unbeaten record intact, while the Atlas Lions aim to extend their remarkable tournament run against one of the competition's elite sides in a 2022 rematch. Follow our liveblog for minute-by-minute commentary.

events.title: Mali rebels attack convoy of Russian reinforcements headed to besieged military camp
events.summary: Tuareg separatists from the Azawad Liberation Front and their jihadist allies attacked a convoy of Russian soldiers and Malian troops Thursday as they were en route to reinforce a military camp in the northern town of Anefis.

events.title: Iran claims US strike hit near nuclear powerplant
events.summary: The United States and Iran are continuing to trade strikes on July 9 for a second straight day. after President Donald Trump said the ceasefire between the two countries was "dead". According to Iran's health ministry, the two days of US attacks have killed at least 14 people and injured more than 70. In response, Tehran said it had resumed its targeting of US assets in Kuwait, Bahrain and Qatar.

events.title: Turkey: Erdogan gives NATO leaders pistols with ammunition after summit
events.summary: NATO leaders received a vintage revolver, along with a box of ammunition, from Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan after Wednesday's summit in Ankara.

events.title: Why are Macron's aviator glasses in the spotlight again?
events.summary: The French president has once again been spotted wearing sunglasses indoors. On this edition of Entre Nous, we look at what’s behind the shades: why he wears them, what they reveal about France’s eyewear industry, and why another type of glasses – smart glasses – is having a moment too.

events.title: 'Total Eclipse of the Heart' singer and 80s power ballad queen Bonnie Tyler dies aged 75
events.summary: Best known her power ballad “Total Eclipse of the Heart” that topped the charts in 1983, Bonnie Tyler, the gravelly voiced, Grammy-nominated Welsh pop star has died. She was 75 and passed away in hospital in Portugal from an illness, after undergoing emergency intestinal surgery in May. Her signature voice was actually the result of an accident after not resting enough following an operation to remove nodules on her vocal cords.

events.title: Farage to face Count Binface in self-triggered byelection amid funding probe
events.summary: British anti-immigrant politician Nigel Farage faces the embarrassing prospect of going head-to-head with perennial joke candidate Count Binface in a by-election he called himself. The shock announcement came as Farage is the subject of a parliamentary probe over the non-disclosure of a £5 million donation from Thailand-based cryptocurrency billionaire Christopher Harborne. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali tells us more.

events.title: As Iran buries Khamenei, it shifts from 'a theocracy to a nationalistic military leadership'
events.summary: Khamenei's marathon funeral is coming to an end as the coffin arrives in Mashhad, following a week of mass processions across Iran resonating with revenge slogans against Trump. It comes as strikes between of US and Iran have started up again. The funeral is also being used as a way to project to the world a regime that has managed to withstand the war. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.

events.title: 'Kylian, forgive us': How the World Cup turned Mbappé's reputation around
events.summary: It seems everyone loves Kylian Mbappé again. The France captain, is having a magnificent showing in the World Cup, scoring seven goals so far and breaking records. His performance has led some who previously criticized him for being too outspoken, or for leaving Paris to play in Madrid, to ask for forgiveness.

events.title: Mauritania's fishing heritage threatened by climate change and overfishing
events.summary: For centuries, the Imraguen people of Mauritania have relied on wind-powered boats and traditional fishing methods to live in harmony with the rich waters of the Banc d’Arguin. But climate change, overfishing and the gradual loss of ancestral knowledge are threatening both their livelihoods and a unique cultural heritage.

events.title: Militia-like movements appear in Chad amid surge in intercommunal violence
events.summary: In Chad, intercommunal violence has dramatically risen, with authorities flagging five different incidents just last month, with over 300 killed in just two years. The UN has warned that violence is escalating across the entire Lake Chad basin, with 5,700 people killed since last November. Violence has also forced thousands to leave. FRANCE 24's Harold Girard tells us more

events.title: Nigerian police under pressure to reform force amid public distrust
events.summary: The 36 states of the Federation have yet to ratify the establishment of a State Police force under their responsibility. For several decades, Nigeria has been struggling to restructure the architecture of its internal security: But with a national police force and a local police force, this would be one of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s responses as the overall security situation in Nigeria continues to deteriorate. FRANCE 24's

events.title: Nigeria probes made-up 'presidential' agency that secured thousands in public funds
events.summary: Nigeria is investigating a made-up government agency which authorities are saying was cooked up by a conman, and somehow managed to operate under authorities' noses. It had set up offices on government premises and even got itself listed on the budget for allocation of public money. It was supposed to attract foreign investment to Nigeria, instead drawing criticism on how the administration manages its house.

events.title: Africa CDC warns of ‘fastest-growing Ebola outbreak ever’ as DR Congo death toll climbs to 600
events.summary: The number of confirmed deaths in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has risen to 600, figures published by the UN health agency showed Thursday. The WHO's figures show the worsening haemorrhagic fever outbreak has a case fatality rate of 34 percent.

events.title: 'Lucky', 'Ted Lasso' and 'Little House on the Prairie': TV series to watch this summer
events.summary: From Netflix’s ambitious reboot of “Little House on the Prairie” to Anya Taylor-Joy’s high-stakes crime thriller “Lucky”, this summer’s TV line-up has something for every viewer. We also preview “GIGN” (Elite Force), a gripping French action series that takes viewers inside one of the world’s most elite police units. Fan favourites are returning too, with a new season of “Ted Lasso” and Will Ferrell taking on his first major TV leading role as an ageing golf champion in The Hawk. Arts24’s Dheepthika Laurent rounds up the biggest series to add to your watchlist this summer.

events.title: World Cup 2026: France and Morocco prepare for quarter-final clash
events.summary: For the second World Cup in a row, France and Morocco meet again in the knockout stage. Both teams are preparing for another intense battle, with several players on either side having shared the dressing room at Paris Saint-Germain. Didier Deschamps has handed Desiré Doué the task of taking on Achraf Hakimi in what promises to be one of the tournament's most eagerly anticipated quarter-finals.

events.title: La Vie en Paws: Inside France's love affair with pets
events.summary: This week, we take a deep dive into a love affair that rivals France’s passion for wine, cheese and fashion: the French and their pets. Whether furry, feathered or scaly, the affection runs deep. More than half of French households own at least one pet, adding up to an astonishing 75 million animals across the country – meaning pets actually outnumber people in France! While many owners lavish attention on their animal companions, helping drive a booming pet care industry, there is also a darker side: some pets are abandoned, particularly during the summer months.

events.title: Protests threaten to spiral out of control after rape and murder of girl, 12, in India
events.summary: Violent protests are threating to spiral out of control in India after the rape and murder of a 12-year-old girl.

events.title: Energy costs rise and stocks fall sharply as US-Iran peace is shattered
events.summary: There has been a marked increase in global energy prices amid fears of a prolonged resumption to hostilities between the US and Iran.

events.title: China braces for super typhoon after severe flooding and thunderstorms leave multiple people dead
events.summary: Severe flooding that hit southern China earlier this week could expand to other provinces with the arrival of Super Typhoon Bavi at the weekend.

events.title: Ten children among at least 13 killed in separate Bangladesh landslides
events.summary: At least five children have died in a landslide caused by heavy monsoon rains in Bangladesh, two days after eight people, including five children, died in a separate landslide in the country.

events.title: Mexican village that warned of cartel offensive during World Cup faces drone attacks
events.summary: Residents of the rural community of Guajes de Ayala in Mexico have faced a violent attack by the La Nueva Familia Michoacana cartel

events.title: Chinese region reels from floods and destruction from remnants of tropical storm
events.summary: China has allocated millions in relief funding for disaster-hit areas after severe storms and a deadly landslide

events.title: Pakistani rescuers battle rough seas on 2nd day of search for cargo plane crew
events.summary: Officials say Pakistan Navy search and rescue teams are battling rough seas as they try to find the five-member crew of a cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea

events.title: Fire at a shoe factory kills 28 in one of China's deadliest blazes in recent years
events.summary: A fire has broken out at a shoe factory in China's Fujian province, killing 28 people

events.title: Health workers in Congo go on strike over pay issues as deaths near 600
events.summary: Healthcare workers at the epicenter of Congo’s Ebola outbreak are walking off their jobs to protest delays in their payments

events.title: What to know about Nigel Farage's dramatic resignation and political gambit
events.summary: Nigel Farage claims the political establishment is out to stop him

events.title: UN urge Western nations to engage with Afghanistan to prevent instability
events.summary: Two top United Nations officials are urging Western nations to engage with Afghanistan to prevent the country from sliding back into instability

events.title: Bus collision with truck leaves 14 dead in remote area of northern Uganda
events.summary: At least 14 people have died and 28 others were injured in a remote area of northern Uganda after a bus was in collision with a truck, according to police

events.title: With Le Pen sentencing, France's presidential election veers into the extraordinary
events.summary: France's upcoming election has taken a striking turn after far-right leader Marine Le Pen’s decision to run for the presidency for a fourth time

events.title: Meta plans billions for first AI data center in Canada, largest outside the US
events.summary: Facebook and Instagram parent Meta said Wednesday it will invest more than 13 billion Canadian dollars ($9.1 billion) to build its first artificial intelligence data center in Canada and its largest outside the United States

events.title: Technology is driving an increase in online threats to the UK, senior officials say
events.summary: Senior British policing officials say technology and online platforms are increasingly being exploited for threats to the United Kingdom

events.title: Telegram used to share rape videos and drugging tips, prosecutors say
events.summary: A German investigation into an online predator network that thrived on the messaging app Telegram for years has led to the convictions of three alleged members of the group's inner circle and the ongoing trial of a fourth man in Berlin

events.title: Orbán allies protest in Hungary against plans to oust President Tamás Sulyok
events.summary: Opponents of the Hungarian government's efforts to oust the president have gathered for a protest in Budapest at the urging of former Prime Minister Viktor Orbán

events.title: Berlin court sentences a doctor to life for murdering 15 patients under palliative care
events.summary: A Berlin court has convicted and sentenced a German doctor to life in prison on charges of murdering 15 of his patients who were under palliative care

events.title: Turkey's Erdogan hosts NATO summit as political rival Imamoglu defends himself in court
events.summary: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is hosting NATO leaders in Ankara on Wednesday, while his main political rival faces corruption charges in court

events.title: Funeral processions held in holy Iraqi cities for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
events.summary: Funeral processions for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei have been held in Iraq's holy cities of Najaf and Karbala

events.title: Bangladesh relocates refugees after landslide kills at least 5 children
events.summary: Authorities in Rohingya refugee camps in Bangladesh are using loudspeakers and a network of volunteers and community leaders to relocate people from risky areas to safety

events.title: Canada's Carney visits Saudi Arabia as the prime minister seeks to expand ties with kingdom
events.summary: Canada's prime minister is visiting Saudi Arabia, marking the first trip by a Canadian leader to the kingdom in 26 years

events.title: Russian attacks kill 3 across Ukraine, strikes Kyiv for second consecutive night
events.summary: Russian drone and missile attacks across Ukraine have killed three people, including one in Kyiv, during a second consecutive night of strikes on the capital

events.title: Pakistan expands search for missing cargo plane as rough seas hamper rescue efforts
events.summary: Pakistan’s Navy and civilian authorities have expanded the search for a cargo plane feared to have crashed after it disappeared from radar and lost contact with air traffic control en route to the city of Karachi

events.title: Rescuers search for 5 missing people after a deadly landslide in India's Kerala state
events.summary: Rescue teams in the southern Indian state of Kerala are racing to find five people still missing a day after heavy monsoon rains caused a deadly landslide

events.title: Australia agrees to sell uranium to India, ending a long stalemate
events.summary: Australia has agreed to sell uranium to India for peaceful purposes

events.summary: Hansen was the sole Canadian astronaut to take part in NASA’s Artemis II mission in April.

events.title: Trump ally and hard-right leader Nigel Farage triggers vote in bid to clear name amid financial scrutiny
events.summary: “This will be a people-versus-the establishment by-election,” Farage said. He was instrumental in Britain’s decision to leave the European Union in 2016.

events.title: French court allows far-right leader Marine Le Pen to run for president with ankle tag
events.summary: An appeals court shortened the ban on Le Pen’s holding public office. But it ordered her to wear an electronic monitoring tag for a year, making a presidential campaign politically and logistically difficult.

events.title: China conducts rare long-range missile test, rattling U.S. allies
events.summary: The strategic missile was fired into the Pacific from a nuclear-powered submarine, state media reported, in the Chinese military’s first such test since 2024.

events.title: DOJ defends decision to drop criminal charges against billionaire Indian businessman
events.summary: The government says its decision to dismiss its case against Gautam Adani, one of the world’s wealthiest people, wasn’t swayed by his reported offer to invest in the U.S.

events.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body ahead of power transfer
events.summary: The move was largely symbolic as the militant group prepares to transfer authority, with no clear mention of plans for disarmament.

events.title: Supreme leader’s coffin creeps through crowds in Tehran as Trump effigy is hanged
events.summary: Authorities have shut down streets, airspace and daily life for the mourning, which began Saturday and will end Thursday as Khamenei, 86, is buried at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace.

events.title: Venezuela’s earthquake death toll rises to more than 3,500 as survivors look for relatives
events.summary: As the number of deaths increased Monday, survivors like 12-year-old Fabiana Blanco say they have been left with “a battle scar.”

events.title: Deadly Russian strikes hit Kyiv on eve of Trump trip to NATO summit
events.summary: The attack, which Ukraine’s president had warned of hours earlier, comes days after a combined Russian assault killed at least 31 people in Kyiv.

events.title: England’s Jordan Henderson seriously injured during ‘Wonderwall’ celebration after World Cup win
events.summary: The Brentford midfielder had to be taken off the pitch on a stretcher after he jumped over a short wall and landed awkwardly.

events.title: Prince Harry’s U.K. trip sparks fresh dispute over Buckingham Palace stay
events.summary: The latest twist is sparking further speculation about the acrimonious relationship between King Charles III and his wayward son.

events.title: Chinese pastor released from prison after Trump raised his case with Xi Jinping
events.summary: Ezra Jin Mingri, who founded Beijing’s Zion Church, was detained in October in one of China’s largest crackdowns on a single church in decades.
